title: 2012. évi C. törvény a Büntető Törvénykönyvről — Informatikai bűncselekmények
title_en: Act C of 2012 on the Criminal Code — Cybercrime Provisions
description: Cybercrime and computer-related offences from the Hungarian Criminal Code (Büntető Törvénykönyv, Btk.). Covers information system fraud, unauthorised access, data interference, system interference, and misuse of devices. Transposing Directive 2013/40/EU on attacks against information systems.

provisions.title: § 375 — Information System Fraud (Információs rendszer felhasználásával elkövetett csalás)
provisions.content: Any person who, with the intention of obtaining an unlawful financial gain for himself or another, introduces data into an information system, alters, deletes or renders inaccessible data processed in an information system, or otherwise interferes with the functioning of an information system, and thereby causes financial damage, commits the offence of information system fraud. The punishment shall be imprisonment for up to three years. If the offence results in significant financial damage (exceeding five million forints), the punishment shall be imprisonment between one and five years. If the offence results in particularly significant financial damage (exceeding fifty million forints), the punishment shall be imprisonment between two and eight years. If the offence is committed as part of a criminal organisation, the punishment shall be imprisonment between five and ten years.

provisions.title: § 422 — Unauthorised Access to an Information System (Információs rendszer vagy adat megsértése)
provisions.content: Any person who gains unauthorised access to an information system by breaching or circumventing the protective measures of the system commits the offence of unauthorised access. The punishment shall be imprisonment for up to two years. If the offence is committed against an information system operated by a public body, a financial institution, or a critical infrastructure operator, the punishment shall be imprisonment for up to three years. If the offence results in a significant disruption of the system or in the compromise of a large quantity of personal data, the punishment shall be imprisonment between one and five years. This provision implements Article 3 of Directive 2013/40/EU on attacks against information systems.

provisions.title: § 423 — Data Interference (Információs rendszerben tárolt adatok megsértése)
provisions.content: Any person who, without authorisation, deletes, damages, alters, or renders inaccessible data stored in or transmitted by an information system commits the offence of data interference. The punishment shall be imprisonment for up to two years. If the data affected is personal data of a special category or classified information, the punishment shall be imprisonment between one and five years. If the offence causes significant damage to the operation of an essential service, the punishment shall be imprisonment between two and eight years. This provision implements Article 5 of Directive 2013/40/EU on attacks against information systems.

provisions.title: § 423/A — System Interference (Információs rendszer működésének megzavarása)
provisions.content: Any person who, without authorisation, seriously hinders or interrupts the functioning of an information system by: (a) inputting data; (b) transmitting, damaging, deleting, deteriorating, altering or suppressing data; (c) rendering data inaccessible; or (d) carrying out any other operation that disrupts the system, commits the offence of system interference. The punishment shall be imprisonment for up to three years. If the system affected is a critical infrastructure or provides an essential service, the punishment shall be imprisonment between one and five years. If the offence is committed by means of a botnet or results in particularly significant damage, the punishment shall be imprisonment between two and eight years. This provision implements Article 4 of Directive 2013/40/EU on attacks against information systems.

provisions.title: § 424 — Misuse of Devices (Információs rendszer védelmét biztosító technikai intézkedés kijátszása)
provisions.content: Any person who produces, sells, procures for use, imports, distributes, or otherwise makes available: (a) a device, including a computer program, designed or adapted primarily for the purpose of committing any of the offences defined in Sections 422 to 423/A; (b) a computer password, access code or similar data by which an information system is capable of being accessed, without authorisation and with the intention that it be used for the purpose of committing any offence under Sections 422 to 423/A, commits the offence of misuse of devices. The punishment shall be imprisonment for up to two years. Possession of such devices or data with the intention of committing an offence under Sections 422 to 423/A shall be punishable by imprisonment for up to one year. This provision implements Article 7 of Directive 2013/40/EU on attacks against information systems.

provisions.title: § 375/A — Phishing and Identity Theft
provisions.content: Any person who, by means of false or misleading electronic communication or by creating a fraudulent information system interface, induces another person to disclose personal identification data, authentication credentials, financial account data, or other data that enables access to an information system or financial service, commits the offence of online identity fraud. The punishment shall be imprisonment for up to three years. If the offence is committed on a large scale affecting a significant number of victims, the punishment shall be imprisonment between one and five years.

provisions.title: § 375/B — Ransomware
provisions.content: Any person who renders the data or system of another inaccessible or threatens to disclose, destroy or render inaccessible data unless a financial payment is made, using an information system, commits the offence of digital extortion. The punishment shall be imprisonment between one and five years. If the offence targets a critical infrastructure, a health institution, or an educational institution, the punishment shall be imprisonment between two and eight years. If the offence is committed as part of a criminal organisation, the punishment shall be imprisonment between five and ten years.

provisions.title: § 422/A — Illegal Interception
provisions.content: Any person who, without authorisation, intercepts, by technical means, non-public transmissions of data to, from or within an information system, including electromagnetic emissions from an information system carrying such data, commits the offence of illegal interception. The punishment shall be imprisonment for up to three years. If the data intercepted includes classified information or special categories of personal data, the punishment shall be imprisonment between one and five years. This provision implements Article 6 of Directive 2013/40/EU on attacks against information systems.

provisions.title: § 137 — Attack Against Critical Information Infrastructure
provisions.content: Any person who commits an offence under Sections 422 to 424 against an information system designated as part of the national critical information infrastructure commits an aggravated offence. The punishment shall be the maximum applicable to the underlying offence increased by one-half, but shall not exceed twenty years of imprisonment. Critical information infrastructure includes systems essential for energy supply, water management, healthcare, transport, telecommunications, and financial services.

provisions.title: § 176 — Misuse of Personal Data
provisions.content: Any person who, for the purpose of causing harm, unlawfully processes personal data or who fails to take the security measures required for the protection of personal data, and thereby causes significant harm to the data subject, commits the offence of misuse of personal data. The punishment shall be imprisonment for up to one year. If the offence involves special categories of personal data or the personal data of a large number of persons, the punishment shall be imprisonment for up to two years.

provisions.title: § 300 — Breach of Secrecy of Correspondence in Electronic Communications
provisions.content: Any person who, without authorisation, opens, obtains or discloses the content of an electronic communication of another person, commits the offence of breach of secrecy of correspondence. The punishment shall be imprisonment for up to one year. If the offence is committed by a person entrusted with the transmission of the communication (such as a service provider employee), the punishment shall be imprisonment for up to two years.

provisions.title: § 418 — Copyright Infringement by Electronic Means
provisions.content: Any person who, by electronic means and on a commercial scale, reproduces or makes available to the public a copyright-protected work without the authorisation of the right holder, commits the offence of copyright infringement. The punishment shall be imprisonment for up to two years. If the infringement involves a particularly large number of works or causes significant financial damage to the right holder, the punishment shall be imprisonment between one and five years.

provisions.title: § 459 — Interpretive Provisions
provisions.content: For the purposes of this Act: (a) 'information system' means any device or group of interconnected or related devices, one or more of which performs automatic processing of data pursuant to a program; (b) 'data' includes any representation of facts, information or concepts in a form suitable for processing in an information system; (c) 'critical infrastructure' means an asset or system essential for the maintenance of vital societal functions, health, safety, security, economic or social well-being, the disruption or destruction of which would have significant impact in Hungary; (d) 'significant damage' means damage exceeding five hundred thousand forints.

provisions.title: § 460 — Jurisdiction
provisions.content: Hungarian criminal law shall apply to offences committed through information systems where: (a) the perpetrator or the victim is a Hungarian citizen; (b) the information system affected is located in Hungary; (c) the act was committed from the territory of Hungary; or (d) the act has a significant effect in Hungary. These jurisdictional provisions ensure effective prosecution of cybercrime in accordance with Articles 12-13 of Directive 2013/40/EU on attacks against information systems.

provisions.title: § 461 — Aggravating Circumstances
provisions.content: The punishment for offences under Sections 375 and 422 to 424 shall be increased if the offence: (a) is committed as part of a criminal organisation; (b) causes particularly significant damage (exceeding fifty million forints); (c) affects the personal data of a large number of persons (one thousand or more); (d) targets a system that is part of the national critical infrastructure; (e) is committed during a state of emergency or crisis. The increase shall be one-half of the maximum sentence otherwise applicable.

provisions.title: § 462 — International Cooperation
provisions.content: The Hungarian authorities shall cooperate with the competent authorities of other EU Member States and third countries in the investigation and prosecution of cybercrime offences. Such cooperation shall include mutual legal assistance, joint investigation teams, and the exchange of information through established channels including Europol, Eurojust, and the European Judicial Network. The 24/7 contact point for international cooperation on cybercrime shall be designated by the Government in accordance with the Budapest Convention on Cybercrime.

definitions.definition: Any device or group of interconnected or related devices, one or more of which performs automatic processing of data pursuant to a program.
An asset or system essential for the maintenance of vital societal functions, whose disruption or destruction would have significant impact in Hungary.
Damage exceeding five hundred thousand forints.
